# UX Checklist: Command, workflow, manifest, and file-layout reference

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-007 requirements and planning artifacts define a scannable public reference experience for generated pages, sidebar navigation, deep links, and Claude Code/Codex comparisons.
**Created**: 2026-06-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the seven generated reference subpages defined with stable public routes and checked-in output paths? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, FR-017]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the `/reference/` landing page, Reference sidebar group, generated subpage order, and glossary position all specified? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, FR-019]
- [ ] CHK003 Are generated full-page content blocks specified with reader-facing purpose, platform mapping, source facts, sources, and inferred notes instead of a raw file dump? [Completeness, Spec FR-016, Session 2026-06-17 - Generation Format And Reviewability]
- [ ] CHK004 Are context-specific deep-link requirements defined for existing install, first-run, troubleshooting, security, and contributor pages to the generated reference subpages rather than only the `/reference/` landing page? [Completeness, Spec FR-023, Session 2026-06-17 - IA And Route Shape]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is "public-readable" generated prose clarified with concrete Markdown structure that reviewers can inspect in diffs? [Clarity, Spec FR-016, Plan Summary]
- [ ] CHK006 Are Claude Code and Codex surface mappings required to stay parallel while preserving runtime-specific differences? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, FR-007, Data Model PlatformMapping]
- [ ] CHK007 Are public link shapes, trailing slashes, and source file locations stated consistently for every generated subpage? [Clarity, Session 2026-06-17 - IA And Route Shape]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK008 Do the requested existing-page deep-link expectations align with the plan's declared file operations and scope budget? [Consistency, Plan Declared File Operations, Plan Reviewability Budget]
- [ ] CHK009 Do the generated-page readability requirements align between the spec, data model, contract, and quickstart? [Consistency, Spec FR-016, Data Model ReferenceRecord, Contract Content Contract, Quickstart Generate Reference Pages]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK010 Are navigation outcomes measurable for reaching generated subpages from the landing page or Reference sidebar group? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-001]
- [ ] CHK011 Are citation and source/inference separation outcomes measurable for generated rows? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-002, SC-003]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK012 Are inbound `/reference/` links preserved while generated subpages are added below the existing landing page? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK013 Are optional or absent repository surfaces addressed without inventing source facts? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, FR-004, FR-014]

## Verification Rerun - 2026-06-17

- [ ] CHK014 Are existing-page deep-link target families specified by page type so the implementation can avoid generic-only `/reference/` links? [Completeness, Spec FR-023, Session 2026-06-17 - IA And Route Shape]
- [ ] CHK015 Are link-only updates to the existing install, first-run, troubleshooting, security, and contributor pages included in declared file operations and scope budget? [Consistency, Plan Declared File Operations, Plan Reviewability Budget]
- [ ] CHK016 Are DOC-008 troubleshooting/security depth and DOC-009 contributor/release procedure depth still excluded while allowing DOC-007 reference links? [Scope, Spec FR-018, Spec Assumptions, Plan Generated Output Declaration]
- [ ] CHK017 Are generated full-page readability requirements still explicit after adding existing-page deep links? [Clarity, Spec FR-016, Contract Content Contract]
